facts.text: Poetry magazine, founded in 1912 by Harriet Monroe while she worked as a Chicago Tribune art critic, began as a literary voice.
The 1893 World’s Fair drew 27.5 million visitors, cementing Chicago’s reputation as a hub of innovation and design.
Jean Baptiste Point du Sable, a free black trader from Haiti, became Chicago’s first permanent non‑indigenous resident in the 1770s.
The Willis Tower, once the world’s tallest building, remains a skyline icon and a symbol of Chicago’s architectural ambition.
Chicago hosts the world‑renowned Symphony Orchestra and the Lyric Opera, showcasing the city’s enduring commitment to classical music.
The Chicago Bears, one of the NFL’s oldest teams, have claimed nine championships, including the 1985 Super Bowl XX victory.
